---
title: "Schema & Variable Dictionary"
author: "World Bank / UNHCR Joint Team"
date: today
---

## Overview

Every harmonized file uploaded and approved through the pipeline must conform to
this schema. Variables are grouped into **mandatory** (must be present and
non-missing per the rules of the variable) and **optional** (included when
available in the source survey).

The **unit of observation** is the individual: one row per person, with
household-level variables (e.g. `welfare`, `weight`, `hhid`) repeated across all
members of the same household.

## Conventions

- **Type** refers to the Stata storage type of the variable in the harmonized
  `.dta` file: `str#` (string), `byte`/`int`/`long` (integers), `float`/`double`
  (reals). Read the equivalents in R (`character`, `integer`, `double`) via
  [`haven`](https://haven.tidyverse.org).
- **Missing** â€” mandatory variables may not be missing except where noted. Use
  Stata's system missing (`.`) for genuinely unknown values; do not use numeric
  sentinels such as `-9` or `99`.
- **Labels** â€” categorical variables must carry Stata value labels; the numeric
  codes below are authoritative and must match across all surveys.
- **Identifiers** are strings to preserve leading zeros and avoid precision loss.

## Mandatory Variables

| Variable | Label | Type | Unit / range | Missing | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `hhid` | Household identifier | `str` | â€” | Not allowed | Unique per household within the survey; string to preserve leading zeros |
| `pid` | Person identifier | `str` | â€” | Not allowed | Unique per individual within the survey |
| `welfare` | Welfare aggregate | `double` | â‰Ą 0, LCU, annual per capita | Not allowed | Consumption or income aggregate from the survey's official poverty methodology; `welfare_type` records which |
| `welfare_type` | Welfare measure type | `byte` | `1`â€“`3` | Not allowed | `1` = Consumption, `2` = Income, `3` = Expenditure |
| `welfare_self` | Self-reliance-adjusted welfare | `double` | â‰Ą 0, LCU, annual per capita | Not allowed | Welfare measure used for the 50by30 self-reliance indicator |
| `weight` | Sampling weight | `double` | > 0 | Not allowed | Individual sampling/population weight |
| `weight_type` | Weight type | `byte` | `1`â€“`2` | Not allowed | `1` = Frequency weight (FW), `2` = Probability weight (PW) |
| `hsize` | Household size | `int` | â‰Ą 1 | Not allowed | Number of household members |
| `countrycode` | Country code | `str3` | ISO / WDI 3-letter | Not allowed | e.g. `AGO`, `ETH` |
| `year` | Survey year | `int` | 1990â€“2035 | Not allowed | Four-digit year of data collection |

## Optional Variables

| Variable | Label | Type | Unit / range | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `age` | Age | `double` | 0â€“120 years | Decimal allowed for children under 5 (per GMD 2.0); integer otherwise |
| `male` | Sex | `byte` | `0`â€“`1` | `0` = Female, `1` = Male |
| `urban` | Urban/rural | `byte` | `0`â€“`1` | `0` = Rural, `1` = Urban |
| `educat4` | Education (4 cat.) | `byte` | `1`â€“`4` | See value labels below |
| `empstat` | Employment status | `byte` | `1`â€“`4` | `1` = Employed, `2` = Unemployed, `3` = Out of labor force, `4` = Not applicable |
| `strata` | Sampling strata | `int` | â€” | Design variable for variance estimation |
| `psu` | Primary sampling unit | `long` | â€” | Design variable for variance estimation |

## Value Labels

Categorical variables must use these codes and labels exactly.

`welfare_type`

: `1` Consumption Â· `2` Income Â· `3` Expenditure

`weight_type`

: `1` Frequency weight (FW) Â· `2` Probability weight (PW)

`male`

: `0` Female Â· `1` Male

`urban`

: `0` Rural Â· `1` Urban

`educat4`

: `1` No education Â· `2` Primary Â· `3` Secondary Â· `4` Tertiary

`empstat`

: `1` Employed Â· `2` Unemployed Â· `3` Out of labor force Â· `4` Not applicable

::: {.bg-light-yellow .pa3 .br2 .f6}
**Status:** This variable dictionary is a working draft. Confirm the final variable
list, value-label codes, and the precise definition of `welfare_self` with the
50by30 methodology team before treating it as authoritative.
:::
